behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between manifest validation and plugin execution pipeline (cross-module handoff/order), not a single pure function. Rejected higher level: no real HTTP/subprocess/external API or AI output in behavior text, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified. Rejected lower level: behavior text requires proving 'before any code ... is executed,' which a standalone validator unit test cannot guarantee.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with an invalid manifest and an entry module that writes a side-effect marker if executed. Run the real loader wiring (manifest scan + validation + load). Assert loader reports manifest rejection and side-effect marker is absent.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly tests dependency-resolution order across plugins and lifecycle invoker, which is module orchestration. Rejected higher level: no subprocess/network/OS-primitive dependence beyond local module/file loading; no AI model output; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Set up two real plugin packages in a temp directory: A depends on B. Each onLoad appends its name to a shared in-memory/file log. Run loader and assert log order is [B, A].

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection outcome must propagate to load suppression for both plugins (resolver + executor coordination). Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require subprocess/network/external APIs or AI decisions. Rejected lower level: a unit test of graph cycle detection alone would miss 'loads neither' enforcement in loader orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependencies and onLoad side effects. Run loader. Assert cycle error/report is emitted and neither plugin side effect occurred.

behaviors.description: A plugin's onLoad hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering contract between module import execution and lifecycle hook dispatch, requiring multiple runtime components. Rejected higher level: no requirement for subprocess or external infrastructure; local module import is sufficient. Rejected lower level: unit-testing hook dispatcher alone cannot prove real import-execution-before-hook sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Build a plugin whose entry module sets a flag as top-level side effect and whose onLoad asserts the flag is already set (or records observed state). Run loader and assert onLoad ran and observed post-import state.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its onLoad hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is fault-isolation and dependency-aware continuation across plugin graph stages (error handling + scheduler/orchestrator). Rejected higher level: no real network/subprocess/external API or AI-output dependence; single local runtime process suffices. Rejected lower level: unit-testing catch/retry logic alone misses graph-based 'non-dependent plugins still initialize' interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Create three plugins: F (throws in onLoad), D (depends on F), and I (independent). Run loader. Assert F failure is reported, D is skipped/not initialized, and I onLoad still executes successfully.
